feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: You Are NOT The Father! >hoe sobs >man calls her a lying bitch >audience whoops …
entry.content: You Are NOT The Father! >hoe sobs >man calls her a lying bitch >audience whoops and hollers. They don't make entertainment like this anymore.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: From the sick, depraved mind of Robert Eggers...
entry.content: From the sick, depraved mind of Robert Eggers...

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >save your MMO which has being dead for more than 15 years Seriously, why are th…
entry.content: >save your MMO which has being dead for more than 15 years Seriously, why are they doing this? How are they still around? They are relic from an almost defunct time of games from the 2000s and early 2010s. Are servers really cheap or is there some suspiciously wealthy whales still funding dead MMOs?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Fuck man
entry.content: I want to go back in time. Everything is soulless now. We can't even have physical fucking games anymore!!!! And the saddest part is there are faggets out there that grew up in this shitty soulless environment that will defend the corporations that are willing to risk the entire hobby for a little bit of extra money. Idgaf about 99% of games but the fact that I can no longer buy collectible editions of games i do care about is infuriating me.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Should we get the giant 100 foot battering ram that we specifically built for t…
entry.content: >Should we get the giant 100 foot battering ram that we specifically built for the sole purpose of breaching the gates of Minas Tirith? >No try the smaller one first, it might work you never know

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: people accept the army too much. It's weird how people still fall in line and co…
entry.content: people accept the army too much. It's weird how people still fall in line and comply, rank and file to the orders and commands of the military. I just find this interesting because until the military itself changes, any attempt to liberate or free a society of people will falter and fail unless the military changes along with it. The military it still not really liberal in most parts of the world. It's just weird how people feign social progress, only to fall in line rank and file to the demands

entry.title: /int/ International: Found this gem in a book about the 1950s
entry.content: >In advocating for European integration, Kennan broke with Spykman's advice to not encourage an "United States of Europe" for fear that such a power could easily turn not only into a rival but a potential world conqueror. There were murifats genuinely concerned about the EU taking over the world or something lol

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >Rock stations have been playing the same setlist of songs for almost 20 years >…
entry.content: >Rock stations have been playing the same setlist of songs for almost 20 years >Rock Festivals have the same major actos headlining them as they did 15 years ago >The death of the genre in the 2010s means all we can do is milk the shit out of past acts until they retire or die Really sucks growing up in a world where you can't experience the rise of certain bands or even their peaks. Zoomers just have the leftovers and even have to pay astronomical high prices to experience that in concert

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Medieval 3
entry.content: >TEN (10) factions >HRE is a single faction >no Baltics >no Rus >vote which factions won't be DLC >Poland, Castille, Fatimids, Venice. Portugal and Hungary do not have a sure position for release in the base game >small factions like Bulgaria, Serbia, Croatia and Georgia have no hope of winning the vote It's over.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: What an absolute shitshow. It's official: James Cameron is completely out of thi…
entry.content: What an absolute shitshow. It's official: James Cameron is completely out of things to say. This movie is basically the exact same as Avatar 2, except with a new clan and more Spider bullshit. How could a master storyteller waste so much time to make the same movie twice? To be clear, I kinda like 1 and 2, despite their flaws.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: OPSEC DEMONS of the US Military
entry.content: You can buy precise location data of US Military Personnel on the field if you have a credit card, and despite several warnings by multiple security experts, contractors and even by the research division of the fucking Army they haven't chosen to make reforms. >In 2023, the Army paid to have the threat spelled out. Researchers at Duke University—working under a grant from the US Military Academy at West Point—set out to buy data on American service members the way a foreign adversary might. They

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Why didn't anyone shill this to me earlier? This show is amazing. The plot and c…
entry.content: Why didn't anyone shill this to me earlier? This show is amazing. The plot and characters are great, the premise is one of the more interesting takes on AI. It doesn't take itself way too seriously either. Anyone else watch it recently? Maybe I just had low expectations going in, but I love the show.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: ITT: Musicians with strange post-music careers
entry.content: The frontwoman of the twee band Heavely became an economist and got a CBE >Fletcher read economics at St Edmund Hall, Oxford.[4] In 1993, she completed a PhD in economics at Nuffield College, Oxford titled 'Theories of Self-Regulation'. In 2001, she was appointed Chief Economist at the Office of Fair Trading[5] and in 2008 took on the additional role of Senior Director of Mergers.[6] She left in April 2013, to become Professor of Competition Policy at the University of East Anglia.[7] She has be

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: HellHole - Final
entry.content: "Retribution Part 2" First, I want to thank everyone who was in this thread; you made me laugh quite a bit. Now, here is the full version of the video: https://litter.catbox.moe/l84un481v 6w0dbsm.mp4 This one is quite long (6 minutes) and it's the video that took me the longest to make. There's a message for everyone at the end, please read it so this thread doesn't get filled with nothing but insults. And that's it, bye bye ;)

entry.title: /int/ International: Do your culture have this practice? Whenever I visited China, when it came time …
entry.content: Do your culture have this practice? Whenever I visited China, when it came time to pay for the bill at a restaurant, my parents and my uncles/aunts would start fighting for the bill. Like sometimes they would even manhandle/wrestle each other to get to the bill first so they could pay. Also, whenever somebody offered a gift to someone, you'd have to do this whole tango of >take it >no i can't >ahh please just take it >no it's really too much, you shouldn't have >ahh just take it.... repeat 5x un

entry.title: /o/ Auto: New Jeep Cherokee - 0-60 in 6.3 but 5-60 (rolling start) 7.6 engine is 2 boosty …
entry.content: New Jeep Cherokee - 0-60 in 6.3 but 5-60 (rolling start) 7.6 engine is 2 boosty and 2 smol gets pretty good fuel economy but interior is cheap plastic fantastic with only touch controls to adjust the temp, the air suspension gives a firm ride not good! I remember reading a vintage review of a car that had a 2 second gap between 0-60 and 5-60 I wish I could remember what it was just a few days ago. https://www.caranddriver.com/review s/a71536995/2026-jeep-grand-cheroke e-summit-test/ that said, I

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Hi everyone, I'm buying my first car—a 1988 Mazda 626—for $230.
entry.content: Hi everyone. I'm buying my first car—a 1988 Mazda 626—for $230. I live in Russia and decided to go with a Japanese car because I'm a huge fan of their vehicles and culture. This is my first time on 4chan as a participant rather than just an observer, so I'm excited to make my first post. What advice can you give me on getting it roadworthy? The car starts but won't move; I'm having it towed over on the 20th.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: EPP — addressing "retarded LaTeX" and the compression argument
entry.content: been making EPP threads here. previous ones archived. two arguments keep coming up worth addressing properly. %epp=0.3% @heading "EPP vs LaTeX" {bold} @text "plain text. no compiler. reads in notepad." 1. "EPP is a retarded LaTeX with retarded syntax" LaTeX embeds images, needs a compiler, has a Turing-complete macro system and a 4GB install. EPP is plain UTF-8 you can read in notepad and parse in 150 lines. calling EPP a retarded LaTeX is like calling a bicycle a retarded car. also LaTeX suppor

entry.content: /flg/ - F-List General #8351 Hyper Pregnancy Edition Previous Thread: >>83808248 F-list is a website for people interested in erotic roleplaying, and creating character profiles to find like-minded partners with similar interests and fetishes to roleplay with. entry.title: /mu/ Music: Why is death metal said to be the spiritual successor to classical music? It mig…
entry.content: Why is death metal said to be the spiritual successor to classical music? It might be fast, but when you learn the guitar you realise that it is one of the most brainless genres out there. 99% of it is just chugging the bottom two strings with no chord progressions to speak of (for rhythm) and playing scales up and down real fast (for lead). Whatever the fuck the vocalist is doing can't even be called music.

entry.title: /news/ News: Turtle tits-up
entry.content: https://www.yahoo.com/news/us/artic les/ems-called-mitch-mcconnells-hom e-235831905.html Washington — On the same morning Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell was hospitalized last month, emergency medical personnel went to his home to respond to an unconscious person who appeared to experience "cardiac arrest," according to a public EMS dispatch call reviewed by CBS News. During the recording, a dispatcher called in a "cardiac arrest" and a medic said there was "CPR in progress" at McConnell's addre

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: No monsters. No music.
entry.content: I love playing Doom without monsters or music. Just exploring the strange maps and solving puzzles is the one of the most liminal things I've experienced in gaming. You really notice how weird and dreamlike the structures and environments are like this.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Obsidian is also in negotiations with Xbox to avoid closure
entry.content: >Today, the mood is different. Xbox is looking to reallocate where it spends money. The company wants to invest in its big franchises, and to do that, it needs to cut from elsewhere. Studios such as Compulsion, Ninja Theory, Double Fine, Obsidian, Undead Labs and others are deep in negotiation with Microsoft to avoid closure. The impact of this will likely reverberate across the business. >https://www.thegamebusiness.com/p/ xbox-divided-over-its-future-and?tr iedRedirect=true

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Guys, I genuinely think video games may actually die and the point is that we al…
entry.content: Guys, I genuinely think video games may actually die and the point is that we all get pushed into social media and AI for our entertainment options. It's an intentional push. Video games use too many resources and big tech wants all of those resources for AI and monitoring tech. Media streaming will be next.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Why is House MD the only live action adaptation of Sherlock Holmes that understa…
entry.content: Why is House MD the only live action adaptation of Sherlock Holmes that understands his character? He’s a highly observant antisocial freak who only cares about his own mental stimulation. The only reason he’s a detective is because in Victorian England it’s probably the most mentally stimulating job available. If you put him in the modern day with the amount of forensic equipment modern police have he’d probably rather be a murderer because of how much harder and stimulating it is. You can also

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Do i have government malware on my PC?
entry.content: I live in Australia and if i try to use utorrent suddenly my wifi stops working? i can do whatever i want, say whatever i want and nothing, But if i use utorrent suddenly my wifi stops working and the only fix is turning my PC off and on again. Also microsoft processes are taking up like 80% of CPU usage if i hit ctrl alt delete and bring up task manager but as soon as i do that it drops off? pic related best i could catch on it in task manager before it drops off. I feel like this is the govern

entry.title: /news/ News: OPSEC DEMONS in the US Military
entry.content: Del Cameron - May 20, 2026 https://www.wired.com/story/the-pen tagon-knew-enemies-could-track-troo ps-phones-for-years-now-they-are/ For nearly a decade, the Pentagon was warned—by its own contractors, analysts, and intelligence agencies—that anyone with a credit card could buy a map of where American troops sleep, work, and store nuclear weapons. Now the bill has come due in a war zone. A newly disclosed letter shows the warnings went unheeded: US Central Command now confirms it has received “m

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why is Linux so fucking shit at handling audio over bluetooth? Half of the time …
entry.content: Why is Linux so fucking shit at handling audio over bluetooth? Half of the time my headsets will connect in shitty handsfree mode with no option to switch to high quality sound, or connect but have no sound and I have to manually switch to them. I've already tried 50 "solutions" I found online that do nothing so I'm resigned to rolling the dice everytime I connect my headphones.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Modern AI systems such as large language models are fundamentally probabilistic …
entry.content: Modern AI systems such as large language models are fundamentally probabilistic rather than inherently deterministic: at each step, they estimate a probability distribution over possible next outputs and choose from those probabilities. When randomness (sampling) is enabled, the same prompt can produce different valid responses on different runs. However, AI can be made effectively deterministic by fixing the model, inputs, random seed, and decoding settings (for example, using greedy decoding o

entry.content: >Mecha Babe Edition Fapping-focused AIslop thread for those who enjoy plapping to AI thread perfection. A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop.
